jokes.text: Not if it's a song.
jokes.setup: Someone offers to give Jack a gift
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately assumes it's a song and preemptively rejects it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jack's immediate dismissal shows his experience with unwanted employee gifts, particularly musical ones

jokes.text: We rented a villa on St. Esclavage
jokes.setup: Jack describes his exotic wedding location
jokes.punchline: The island name 'St. Esclavage' sounds like 'slavery' in French
jokes.explanation: Brilliant multilingual wordplay that's both dark and sophisticated - classic 30 Rock absurdist naming

jokes.text: an exiled French Admiral/defrocked minister
jokes.setup: Jack needs someone to perform wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Officiant has two completely unrelated disgraced backgrounds
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd 30 Rock character detail - the combination of 'exiled' and 'defrocked' with completely different professions

jokes.text: deep-sea explorer and raconteur Bob Ballard, took ill after eating some bad toucan
jokes.setup: Jack's best man couldn't make it to the wedding
jokes.punchline: Real person Bob Ballard got sick from eating an exotic bird
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack absurdity - name-dropping real explorer, exotic illness, and 'bad toucan' as casual food poisoning

jokes.text: Oh, good God!
jokes.setup: Liz shows Jack their marriage certificate
jokes.punchline: Jack's horrified reaction to seeing he's married to Liz
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat, works because it's Jack's signature exclamation at the right moment

jokes.text: Liz Lemon, I want it to be noted that I am here on time.
jokes.setup: Tracy arrives to work
jokes.punchline: He's proud of basic professional behavior like punctuality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character moment - treating basic adulting as an achievement worthy of recognition

jokes.text: I'm hosting the International Pornography Awards, and I have to go get an insurance physical so I can fly into the arena in a penis-shaped parachute.
jokes.setup: Tracy explains why he has to leave after just arriving
jokes.punchline: Completely insane explanation involving porn awards and penis parachute
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy absurdity - mundane insurance physical connected to completely insane porn award stunt. The escalation from awards to parachute to penis-shaped is perfect

jokes.text: And we have to schedule our rehearsals around the bat mitzvahs Jack has booked in the studio.
jokes.setup: Pete complains about Jack's cost-cutting measures
jokes.punchline: The studio is being rented out for bat mitzvahs
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist premise - professional TV studio being used for bar/bat mitzvahs shows Jack's desperation

jokes.text: When Madison was born, I said, 'Rivka, my schveltz is filled with schpoinkis!'
jokes.setup: Background character giving bar mitzvah speech
jokes.punchline: Made-up Yiddish-sounding gibberish that sounds emotional but means nothing
jokes.explanation: Clever fake Yiddish that sounds authentic but is completely nonsensical - good background gag

jokes.text: Terrible flower girl. At 4 years old, you should know how to pace your petal distribution.
jokes.setup: Liz complaining about wedding chaos
jokes.punchline: She's applying adult professional standards to a 4-year-old's flower girl performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - her neurotic controlling nature applied to a toddler's wedding performance with professional terminology

jokes.text: toiletries, closed-toe shoes, and the State Department-recommended mosquito head net
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her carry-on essentials
jokes.punchline: Escalation from normal items to bureaucratic mosquito protection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - over-prepared with government-approved gear. The specificity makes it land.

jokes.text: Hannah! Petal, step. Petal, step. Come on!
jokes.setup: Following up on flower girl complaint
jokes.punchline: Liz literally trying to coach a 4-year-old mid-ceremony with specific instructions
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the flower girl bit - showing rather than telling Liz's inappropriate coaching of a child

jokes.text: You! It needs to see Mr. Donaghy! It needs to see him right away! Get us all our stuff back!
jokes.setup: Liz mentions problems with monkeys at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Monkeys apparently demanding to see Jack and wanting their stuff back
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist gag - monkeys speaking with formal business language and having legitimate grievances

jokes.text: Well, one of my New Year's resolutions is to say yes... Yes to love, yes to life, yes to staying in more!
jokes.setup: Liz explains why she said 'oui' at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Her inspirational resolution ends with 'staying in more' - the opposite of adventurous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - starting with conventional self-help speak then revealing her true hermit nature

jokes.text: Wife. Mother...
jokes.setup: Jack realizes Liz signed as 'wife' not 'witness'
jokes.punchline: His horrified realization builds as he processes the implications
jokes.explanation: Good escalation beat - Jack's mind going to worst case scenarios in real time

jokes.text: Hitler and Martha Stewart would have hated that wedding!
jokes.setup: Jack complains about the disorganized wedding
jokes.punchline: He equates Hitler and Martha Stewart as both being obsessed with order
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comparison that's both shocking and logically sound - both figures obsessed with control and order

jokes.text: It was a men's tennis shirt and a government-sanctioned head net.
jokes.setup: Jack says minister thought she was the bride in white dress and veil
jokes.punchline: Liz's completely practical, unglamorous description of her 'bridal' outfit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - completely missing the romantic implications, focusing on practical details with bureaucratic language

jokes.text: Who wears shoes on a beach? Only Rocky and Apollo Creed during the training montage!
jokes.setup: Liz wore shoes on the beach
jokes.punchline: Jack's weirdly specific pop culture reference about beach footwear
jokes.explanation: Great Jack character moment - his mind immediately goes to very specific movie reference for any situation

jokes.text: My adventures! I am the protagonist!
jokes.setup: Jack blames Liz for another adventure
jokes.punchline: Liz breaks fourth wall to assert her role as main character
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke where Liz literally asserts her status as the show's protagonist mid-argument

jokes.text: She is not a spy.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions Avery is covering an economic conference in China
jokes.punchline: His unprompted denial that she's a spy implies she probably is
jokes.explanation: Classic 'unprompted denial' comedy - the fact he feels need to clarify suggests the opposite

jokes.text: Whatever you say, dear.
jokes.setup: Jack gives Liz orders about signing divorce papers
jokes.punchline: Liz responds with mock-wifely submission using 'dear'
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Liz sarcastically playing into the marriage situation

jokes.text: Don't forget, honey, we have bridge with the Cunninghams tonight.
jokes.setup: Following the 'dear' comment
jokes.punchline: Liz escalates the fake marriage routine with stereotypical couple plans
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Liz doubling down on fake domestic bliss with specific old-fashioned couple activity

jokes.text: Squeaky Fromme.
jokes.setup: Doctor asks Tracy who he's dating
jokes.punchline: Angie casually mentions dating the infamous Manson family member
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor - casual reference to dating a notorious criminal as if discussing any ex

jokes.text: You have no reflexes, your blood tastes like root beer, and some of your bones appear to have vanished.
jokes.setup: Doctor giving Tracy his physical results
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre and impossible medical findings
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Spaceman absurdity - each medical finding more impossible than the last, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: but my commander refused to pass it on up to Saddam.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman wrote a medical report during Desert Storm
jokes.punchline: He apparently thought he was working for the enemy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dr. Spaceman moment - casually revealing he was confused about which side he was fighting for in a war

jokes.text: Who will be there raise my kids if I'm not around to pay someone to raise my kids?
jokes.setup: Tracy worried about dying and leaving his kids
jokes.punchline: His parenting consists entirely of paying others to parent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character moment - even his paternal concern is filtered through his wealthy celebrity disconnect

jokes.text: We would have asked you sooner, but we forgot you worked here.
jokes.setup: Thanking Danny for taking in Jenna
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that they completely forgot about his existence
jokes.explanation: Brutal deadpan honesty about how forgettable Danny is as an employee

jokes.text: It's just... I got a haircut, and you didn't even notice. Sorry, you get your hair cut every week. Excuse me? I don't like your tone.
jokes.setup: Danny and Jenna moving in together
jokes.punchline: Immediate relationship conflict over haircut appreciation
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy showing how quickly cohabitation creates petty conflicts

jokes.text: I'm not the one who forgot our one-minute anniversary!
jokes.setup: The argument continuing
jokes.punchline: Tracking anniversary in impossibly small increments
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna neediness escalated to absurd precision. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: There's a hygienist there whose boob sometimes touches my ear!
jokes.setup: Frank worried about losing dental insurance
jokes.punchline: His motivation is inappropriate physical contact during dental care
jokes.explanation: Frank character moment - finding inappropriate sexual benefit in medical care, creepy but fits character

jokes.text: He is, dot-dot-dot, my husband!
jokes.setup: Denying Jack is her boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Liz literally says 'dot-dot-dot' instead of pausing for dramatic effect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment - Liz saying the punctuation instead of performing it, very specific to her character

jokes.text: On your wedding night, did he take you in the French fashion?
jokes.setup: Learning about Liz and Jack's marriage
jokes.punchline: Frank's inappropriate sexual question using euphemistic language
jokes.explanation: Frank being inappropriate as usual - fits character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: That's what Ann Curry and Subhas the janitor tried to tell me. That was five years ago.
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentions NBC anti-nepotism rules
jokes.punchline: Apparently Ann Curry has been secretly married to a janitor for years
jokes.explanation: Brilliant background gag - real person Ann Curry in absurd workplace romance, delivered as throwaway detail

jokes.text: They shut down your secret bathroom to save on cleaning costs.
jokes.setup: Pete trying to convince Liz to use leverage
jokes.punchline: The final straw is the loss of Liz's secret bathroom
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - the secret bathroom is such a specific Liz thing that its loss would motivate her

jokes.text: those vegetable chips that I hate and I keep telling Kenneth not to get
jokes.setup: Liz listing what they need
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific complaint about vegetable chips
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that shows Liz's petty grievances even in big moments.

jokes.text: This big red part, you can see, is 'The Biggest Loser.' The yellow slice, our number-two priority... Make It 1997 Again Through Science or Magic.
jokes.setup: Jack showing NBC priority pie chart
jokes.punchline: Second priority is impossible time travel wish
jokes.explanation: Peak 30 Rock absurdity - corporate priority chart with impossible wish as second priority, delivered deadpan

jokes.text: Your show netted $600 last year. Your parents had to buy an ad.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why TGS isn't a priority
jokes.punchline: The show is so unsuccessful that Liz's parents had to buy advertising
jokes.explanation: Brutal and specific insult about the show's financial failure - parents buying ads implies desperate need for any revenue

jokes.text: Well, I've been to a rodeo, too. It was a cat rodeo in a gay guy's apartment.
jokes.setup: Jack says this isn't his first rodeo
jokes.punchline: Liz's rodeo experience is completely bizarre and specific
jokes.explanation: Incredible absurdist Liz moment - taking common expression literally with impossible scenario that's weirdly believable for her life

jokes.text: Oh, my nails are wet!
jokes.setup: Danny and Jenna having domestic fight
jokes.punchline: Jenna's diva excuse for not participating
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna self-absorption moment. Predictable but in character.

jokes.text: After her hysterectomy, she was in a coma briefly.
jokes.setup: Asked how he and his wife don't fight all the time
jokes.punchline: Their peaceful period was when she was literally unconscious from surgery
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny Pete moment - treating medical emergency as relationship advice, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: our children are basically snot silos with BB guns
jokes.setup: Pete explaining how kids serve as buffers
jokes.punchline: Vivid, gross, and accurate description of children
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parenting observation - combining gross bodily functions with dangerous toys in perfect metaphor

jokes.text: Like pigs have around their delicious testicle meat.
jokes.setup: Trying to understand what a buffer is
jokes.punchline: Danny's analogy involves pig anatomy and his enthusiasm for it
jokes.explanation: Good Danny character moment - his mind goes to weird food/anatomy connection, fits his earnest weirdness

jokes.text: my alarm clock didn't go off because it died in a cockfight last night
jokes.setup: Tracy apologizing for being late
jokes.punchline: Impossible excuse involving alarm clock in cockfight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdity - treating inanimate object as living creature in increasingly bizarre scenario.

jokes.text: I'm sorry I'm four hours late, but my alarm clock didn't go off because it died in a cockfight last night.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he's late
jokes.punchline: His alarm clock was apparently a fighting cock that died
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy absurdity - treating living rooster as alarm clock, matter-of-fact delivery of impossible scenario

jokes.text: What? That's double taxation!
jokes.setup: Learning the government takes half of estates
jokes.punchline: Angie's confused outrage using wrong economic terminology
jokes.explanation: Good Angie character moment - she sounds knowledgeable but uses completely wrong economic concept

jokes.text: I mean, look at me! I can't even find Mexico on a map!
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how entertainment rewards lack of talent
jokes.punchline: He admits to basic geographical ignorance as proof
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware moment - Jack admitting his own incompetence while explaining industry success

jokes.text: There's only thing I won't do, and that's take orders from anyone, ever.
jokes.setup: Angie saying she's willing to start at the bottom
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself by refusing the basic requirement of entry-level work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angie contradiction - claiming willingness to start low while rejecting basic workplace hierarchy

jokes.text: I'm not about to cancel my hair appointment with D'Fwan because I'm your new intern.
jokes.setup: Angie starting her internship
jokes.punchline: Immediately prioritizing hair appointment over work
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Angie's priorities. The name D'Fwan adds specificity.

jokes.text: Do you have a problem with strong black women?
jokes.setup: Liz explaining intern job duties
jokes.punchline: Angie immediately plays the race card inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate response from Angie but predictable - fits her combative nature

jokes.text: You're really more like... Oh, boy.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to explain unpaid intern work isn't slavery
jokes.punchline: Her realization mid-sentence that she's about to say something terrible
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Liz realizing in real time she's about to make an offensive comparison

jokes.text: You should hang it on the wall. Careful! Don't coddle the boy. He can do it.
jokes.setup: Kenneth shows them his drawing
jokes.punchline: They treat adult Kenneth like a child, disagreeing on parenting methods
jokes.explanation: Good parenting dynamic applied to adult Kenneth - shows their relationship dysfunction extending to him

jokes.text: But then Subhas and Ann Curry came in, just going to town on...
jokes.setup: Liz hiding in closet to avoid Angie
jokes.punchline: She accidentally witnessed the secret NBC affair in progress
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier gag - showing the consequences of that background relationship

jokes.text: It's my way till payday!
jokes.setup: Jack describing Angie's reality show potential
jokes.punchline: Angie delivers a perfect reality TV catchphrase on cue
jokes.explanation: Perfect reality TV parody - Angie instinctively knows how to create memorable catchphrase

jokes.text: I am friends with a hilarious fat girl and a crazy-eyed, divorced white lady who wants to be in the music business.
jokes.setup: Angie describing her reality show potential
jokes.punchline: Catalog of reality TV stereotypes she has access to
jokes.explanation: Good reality TV casting satire. The specific descriptions hit the stereotypes well.

jokes.text: I agreed to go to a furniture store instead of watching football one time, and I haven't seen a game since.
jokes.setup: Explaining not to back down in marriage
jokes.punchline: One compromise led to total defeat
jokes.explanation: Good marriage advice/horror story. The slippery slope is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: Then you got to hit 'em where it hurts. Find their weakness, and viciously exploit it. That's what marriage is.
jokes.setup: Pete giving Liz advice on fighting Jack
jokes.punchline: His marriage advice is essentially warfare strategy
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate observation about marriage as mutual psychological warfare, delivered as sincere advice

jokes.text: What I don't get is this is Danny's dressing room.
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying he understands people who can't live together
jokes.punchline: Revealing Danny is being kicked out of his own room
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal that recontextualizes the whole breakup. Kenneth's innocent observation lands perfectly.

jokes.text: It's that dark place under the bleachers that's shaped like a wishbone.
jokes.setup: Danny saying he'll stay at the Y
jokes.punchline: His description of the YMCA is weirdly specific and unappealing
jokes.explanation: Danny's earnest but weird description - makes the YMCA sound like a post-apocalyptic shelter

jokes.text: High School for Teen Drama, the Arts, and Feelings
jokes.setup: Announcing fake donation from Jack and Liz
jokes.punchline: School name that sounds like therapy center
jokes.explanation: Good absurd institution name that captures stereotypical liberal arts pretension.

jokes.text: 'cloons', video artists, and theatrical jugglers
jokes.setup: Listing types of artists school will create
jokes.punchline: Made-up word and increasingly useless art forms
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list with invented term 'cloons' and theatrical jugglers as pinnacle of uselessness.

jokes.text: We know what art is! It's paintings of horses!
jokes.setup: Hearing about the arts school announcement
jokes.punchline: Jack's hilariously reductive definition of all art
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - his privileged, conservative view of art reduced to most basic wealthy person cliché

jokes.text: It is my imitation of Drew Barrymore's impression of that crazy lady!
jokes.setup: Asked about her fake voice in the announcement
jokes.punchline: Overly complex explanation of her voice choice involving celebrity impressions
jokes.explanation: Funny meta moment about voice acting choices - the nested impressions are appropriately convoluted for Liz

jokes.text: he rightly pointed out that it made me look like a giant condom.
jokes.setup: Jack taught her not to wear tan-on-tan
jokes.punchline: The specific visual comparison is both accurate and unflattering
jokes.explanation: Vivid and funny visual comparison that's both specific and believable for Liz's fashion sense

jokes.text: Or when you have eye boogers.
jokes.setup: Liz warning Jack when he's too authoritative or handsome
jokes.punchline: Mundane third item deflating the grandiose setup
jokes.explanation: Good deflation comedy - goes from flattering to embarrassing. Simple but effective structure.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Jack. / I apologize, Lemon.
jokes.setup: HR questionnaire revealing their close relationship
jokes.punchline: They apologize in unison, proving the point about thinking alike
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the HR evaluation - they literally demonstrate the 'thinking alike' behavior immediately after it's mentioned

jokes.text: Try to walk like a woman, Lemon. Your fly's open, Jack.
jokes.setup: Leaving the HR office
jokes.punchline: They immediately revert to their usual mutual criticism
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing their relationship immediately returning to normal dynamic

jokes.text: Try to walk like a woman, Lemon. / Your fly's open, Jack.
jokes.setup: End of HR meeting
jokes.punchline: Final exchange of personal critiques
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their relationship dynamic. The mutual embarrassment works as resolution.

jokes.text: I'm D'Fwan! And I just want to be accepted for who I am, a sexual maniac!
jokes.setup: Reality show character introductions
jokes.punchline: D'Fwan's wholesome desire for acceptance paired with inappropriate revelation
jokes.explanation: Good reality TV parody - the contrast between seeking acceptance and oversharing personal details
